name: The Engineers' Testimony
description: Morlak's predecessor kept the twelve confessions and the commissioning document for sixty years. The Crystal Labyrinth records everything within range of its formations — Foss has been studying its oldest recordings. What did the Crystal Labyrinth capture from three hundred years ago?
accept_text: Morlak looks at the open case. He doesn't look at it for long — he's been looking at it for thirty years. 'I have read all twelve confessions,' he says. 'I am not going to tell you what they say because they're not mine to summarize and the context changes what they mean.' He closes the case and fastens it. 'What I'll tell you: the twelve engineers did not know what the commissioning authority intended. They understood they were building a stalemate weapon. They believed it was for tactical advantage in a specific battle. They did not understand it was designed to run for three hundred years.' He meets your eyes. 'Eight of them came to the Wastes to undo what they could. Four did not come. The predecessor's records note that those four were the ones who accepted what they'd been asked to do without asking why.' He is quiet. 'The Crystal Labyrinth. Foss — the researcher there. The Labyrinth's crystal formations have been recording resonance since before the war. If twelve engineers spent months in the Wastes with a three-hundred-year-old resonance device, the Labyrinth captured it.' He sets the sealed case in your hands. 'Take this to Foss. Show her the confessions. Ask her what the Labyrinth recorded when SCAMP discharged. The commissioning document names the authority. I want a second source before I name that name to anyone.'
complete_text: Foss is quiet for a long time after you show her everything. Then she goes to the oldest section of the Labyrinth — the formations that predate the newer survey marks, the ones that have been accumulating resonance recordings since the crystal grew. She listens with her hand on the crystal. She comes back different. 'Three hundred and twenty years ago,' she says. 'A discharge event. The resonance signature of a massive SCAMP detonation — and underneath it, eleven separate human resonance signatures, present for the discharge and absent afterward.' She is looking at the sealed case. 'Eleven. Not twelve.' She meets your eyes. 'One of the engineers' resonance signatures is still in the Labyrinth. Not a recording — a present-tense signature. Someone from the original twelve integrated with the crystal lattice three hundred years ago and is still in the lattice.' She looks at the formations. 'The signature matches the style in the confessions — I can recognize an individual's resonance approach the way you recognize handwriting.' She looks at you. 'One of the engineers who built the Gray Wastes has been inside the crystal lattice for three hundred years. And they're still coherent enough to have a resonance style.' She pauses. 'I think they've been waiting. Specifically waiting. For someone to open the sealed case and come find them.'
